content.speakerText: I feel like such a failure...

options.text: Just cheer up, it's not that bad.
Why do you always think like this?
Maybe you should try harder next time.

content.explanation: 正确答案使用共情+具体回忆来增强信心，符合情绪安抚的核心策略。选项2是空泛安慰，缺乏支持；选项3带有指责语气，会加重负面情绪；选项4暗示对方不够努力，容易引发内疚，均不符合共情原则。

content.speakerText: I just can't handle this stress anymore.
content.speakerTextCn: 我真的受不了这种压力了。

options.text: I'm here for you—this sounds really overwhelming.
Everyone gets stressed, just get over it.
Have you tried meditation or yoga?

content.explanation: 正确答案体现了共情（I'm here for you）和情绪确认（this sounds really overwhelming），是情绪安抚的黄金句式。选项2和4属于否定情绪，具有评判性；选项3虽为建议，但未先处理情绪，属于过早解决问题，不符合先共情后支持的原则。

content.speakerText: I messed up again... I'm such a disappointment.
content.speakerTextCn: 我又搞砸了……我真是个让人失望的人。

options.text: Everyone makes mistakes—it doesn't define you.
You need to be more careful next time.
I know you're trying your best.

content.explanation: 正确答案通过普遍化（Everyone makes mistakes）和去标签化（doesn't define you）减轻自我否定，是高阶共情表达。选项1虽语义正确，但选项3是有效回应，但不如选项1全面；选项2和4属于批评或否定情绪，会加剧自我贬低。

content.hint: 这是回应自我否定时的核心表达，强调成长而非失败
content.knowledgePoint: 成长型思维表达（learning vs. failure）

content.template: I'm here for you—this sounds really ___ .
content.hint: 用于确认对方情绪强度的高频共情词
content.knowledgePoint: 情绪确认表达（overwhelming, tough, hard）

content.template: It's okay to feel this way. ___ you're going through is valid.
content.hint: 用于肯定对方感受的固定结构
content.knowledgePoint: 情感合法性表达（What you're going through is valid）

content.speakerText: I feel like such a failure...
expectedAnswers: I remember how well you handled that big presentation last month. You've got the skills—you're just having a rough moment.
It's okay to feel this way right now. Failure doesn't define you; your effort does.
evaluationCriteria: 是否用共情语言回应，而非否定或轻视情绪
是否使用了具体回忆或事实增强对方信心
是否避免使用空泛安慰（如‘别担心’）或指责性语言（如‘你该更努力’）

content.speakerText: I just can't handle this stress anymore.
content.speakerTextCn: 我真的受不了这种压力了。
expectedAnswers: I'm here for you—this sounds really overwhelming.
It makes sense that you're feeling this way after everything you've been juggling.
You don't have to go through this alone. Would you like to talk about what's weighing on you most?
evaluationCriteria: 是否准确识别并命名对方的情绪（如 overwhelming, stressed）
是否表达支持性陪伴（如 I'm here for you, you're not alone）
是否避免立即给出解决方案，而是先确认情绪

content.speakerText: I messed up again... I'm such a disappointment.
content.speakerTextCn: 我又搞砸了……我真是个让人失望的人。
expectedAnswers: Everyone makes mistakes—it doesn't define you.
I've seen how hard you work. One setback doesn't erase all your progress.
You're being really hard on yourself. What would you say to a friend who felt this way?
evaluationCriteria: 是否帮助对方重新定义自我价值（去标签化）
是否使用对比或事实来挑战负面自我认知
是否引导对方换位思考，促进自我宽恕
